rate_mechanics: Both exchanges calculate funding rates every 8 hours using mark price vs spot index methodology. Gate.io offers lower maker fees at 0.015% compared to Bybit's 0.02%, potentially improving arbitrage profitability. Bybit's superior matching engine and liquidity depth on major pairs typically results in tighter spreads and more predictable funding rate movements. Gate.io's extensive altcoin selection provides more arbitrage opportunities but with higher execution risk on low-volume pairs.
verdict: Bybit excels for high-frequency funding arbitrage on major pairs due to superior infrastructure and liquidity. Gate.io offers better fee structures and broader token coverage for opportunistic arbitrage on emerging altcoins.

faq.question: Which exchange has better funding rate arbitrage opportunities?
faq.answer: Bybit offers more reliable opportunities on major pairs due to higher liquidity and tighter spreads. Gate.io provides more diverse opportunities across 1,700+ pairs but with varying execution quality on smaller tokens.

faq.question: How do the funding rate calculation methods compare?
faq.answer: Both use identical 8-hour intervals and mark price methodology. The key difference is Gate.io's lower maker fees (0.015% vs 0.02%) which directly improves arbitrage profit margins when opening and closing positions.

faq.question: Which platform is better for automated funding arbitrage?
faq.answer: Bybit's reliable API and faster execution make it preferred for automated strategies on liquid pairs. Gate.io's API works well but may experience higher latency during volatile periods, affecting time-sensitive arbitrage execution.

faq.question: What are the main risks when arbitraging between these exchanges?
faq.answer: Cross-exchange arbitrage faces withdrawal delays, network congestion, and basis risk. Bybit's faster processing typically reduces timing risk, while Gate.io's broader selection increases opportunities but also counterparty risk on smaller tokens.
